13480	// AdminGovHospitalApprovalsScreen — approval queue for walk-in Gov/Hospital

13485	function AdminGovHospitalApprovalsScreen({ setScreen, t = T.en, govHospitalBookings = [], setGovHospitalBookings = () => {}, sailings = [], setSailings = () => {} }) {
13486	  const [statusFilter, setStatusFilter] = useState('pending');
13487	  const [dateFilter, setDateFilter] = useState('all');
13488	  const [vesselFilter, setVesselFilter] = useState('all');
13489	  const [rejecting, setRejecting] = useState(null); // booking ref being rejected
13490	  const [rejectReason, setRejectReason] = useState('');
13491	
13492	  // Mockup "today" anchor — submittedAt is stored as "May 30 · HH:MM"; we scope
13493	  // approved/rejected KPI tiles to bookings submitted today.
13494	  const TODAY_PREFIX = 'May 30';
13495	  const submittedToday = (b) => typeof b.submittedAt === 'string' && b.submittedAt.startsWith(TODAY_PREFIX);
13496	
13497	  const pendingCount = govHospitalBookings.filter((b) => b.approvalStatus === 'pending').length;
13498	  const approvedToday = govHospitalBookings.filter((b) => b.approvalStatus === 'approved' && submittedToday(b)).length;
13499	  const rejectedToday = govHospitalBookings.filter((b) => b.approvalStatus === 'rejected' && submittedToday(b)).length;
13500	
13501	  // Pool utilization aggregates across upcoming (non-departed) sailings. Capacity
13502	  // and used (taken + pending) are derived from live sailings.pools state so the
13503	  // tile updates as approvals and walk-in submits land.
13504	  const { utilizationUsed, utilizationCapacity } = sailings.reduce((acc, s) => {
13505	    if (s.departed) return acc;
13506	    for (const cls of ['openair', 'aircon', 'vip']) {
13507	      const gov = s.pools?.[cls]?.govHospital;
13508	      if (!gov) continue;
13509	      acc.utilizationCapacity += gov.capacity || 0;
13510	      acc.utilizationUsed     += (gov.taken || 0) + (gov.pending || 0);
13511	    }
13512	    return acc;
13513	  }, { utilizationUsed: 0, utilizationCapacity: 0 });
13514	  const poolUtilization = `${utilizationUsed}/${utilizationCapacity} seats`;
13515	
13516	  const filtered = govHospitalBookings.filter((b) => {
13517	    if (statusFilter !== 'all' && b.approvalStatus !== statusFilter) return false;
13518	    if (vesselFilter !== 'all' && b.vessel !== vesselFilter) return false;
13519	    return true;
13520	  });
13521	
13522	  // Map a booking record's class label to the sailing.pools key.
13523	  const poolKeyForClass = (label) =>
13524	    label === 'Open Air' ? 'openair' : label === 'Aircon' ? 'aircon' : label === 'VIP' ? 'vip' : null;
13525	
13526	  // Voyage-departure guard: once a sailing has departed the booking's outcome is
13527	  // frozen — approve/reject buttons hide and the row shows a "Voyage departed" lock.
13528	  const sailingById = (id) => sailings.find((s) => s.id === id);
13529	  const isVoyageDeparted = (booking) => Boolean(sailingById(booking?.sailingId)?.departed);
13530	
13531	  // Mutate the matching sailing's gov/hospital pool: approve commits a pending
13532	  // slot (pending--, taken++); reject just releases the slot (pending--).
13533	  // Skips bookings already in a terminal state to keep the action idempotent.
13534	  const mutateGovPool = (booking, action) => {
13535	    const classKey = poolKeyForClass(booking.class);
13536	    if (!classKey) return;
13537	    setSailings((prev) => prev.map((s) => {
13538	      if (s.id !== booking.sailingId) return s;
13539	      const cls = s.pools?.[classKey];
13540	      if (!cls) return s;
13541	      const gov = { ...cls.govHospital };
13542	      const currentPending = gov.pending || 0;
13543	      if (currentPending <= 0) return s; // nothing to release/commit
13544	      gov.pending = currentPending - 1;
13545	      if (action === 'approve') gov.taken = (gov.taken || 0) + 1;
13546	      return { ...s, pools: { ...s.pools, [classKey]: { ...cls, govHospital: gov } } };
13547	    }));
13548	  };
13549	
13550	  const approve = (ref) => {
13551	    const target = govHospitalBookings.find((b) => b.ref === ref);
13552	    if (!target || target.approvalStatus !== 'pending') return;
13553	    if (isVoyageDeparted(target)) return;
13554	    mutateGovPool(target, 'approve');
13555	    setGovHospitalBookings((prev) => prev.map((b) =>
13556	      b.ref === ref ? { ...b, approvalStatus: 'approved', approvedBy: 'Reynaldo Salonga' } : b
13557	    ));
13558	  };
13559	  const openReject = (ref) => {

13561	    if (!target || isVoyageDeparted(target)) return;
13562	    setRejecting(ref); setRejectReason('');

13564	  const confirmReject = () => {

13570	    setGovHospitalBookings((prev) => prev.map((b) =>
13571	      b.ref === rejecting ? { ...b, approvalStatus: 'rejected', rejectionReason: reason } : b

13574	    setRejectReason('');
13575	  };

13796	      {/* Reject modal */}
13797	      {rejecting && (
13798	        <div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center" style={{ background: 'rgba(0,0,0,0.45)' }}>
13799	          <div className="bg-white rounded-2xl p-5 max-w-md w-[90%] border" style={{ borderColor: COLORS.border }}>
13800	            <h3 className="font-bold text-base mb-2" style={{ color: COLORS.ink }}>Reject {rejecting}</h3>
13801	            <p className="text-xs mb-3" style={{ color: COLORS.inkMuted }}>
13802	              Pick a preset reason or type one. The walk-in officer will see this note and the held seat is released back to the pool.
13803	            </p>
13804	            <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 mb-3">
13805	              {['Missing ID', 'Voyage too full', 'Not eligible', 'Duplicate'].map((r) => (
13806	                <button key={r} onClick={() => setRejectReason(r)}
13807	                  className="text-xs font-semibold px-2 py-1 rounded border"
13808	                  style={{ borderColor: rejectReason === r ? COLORS.primary : COLORS.border, color: COLORS.ink, background: rejectReason === r ? '#E0F2FE' : 'white' }}>
13809	                  {r}
13810	                </button>
13811	              ))}
13812	            </div>
13813	            <textarea value={rejectReason} onChange={(e) => setRejectReason(e.target.value)}
13814	              placeholder="Or type a custom reason"
13815	              className="w-full h-20 px-2 py-2 rounded-lg border text-xs"
13816	              style={{ borderColor: COLORS.border, color: COLORS.ink }} />
13817	            <div className="flex gap-2 mt-3 justify-end">
13818	              <button onClick={() => setRejecting(null)} className="text-xs font-semibold px-3 py-2 rounded border" style={{ borderColor: COLORS.border, color: COLORS.ink, background: 'white' }}>Cancel</button>
13819	              <button onClick={confirmReject} className="text-xs font-semibold px-3 py-2 rounded" style={{ background: COLORS.destructive, color: 'white' }}>Confirm rejection</button>
13820	            </div>
13821	          </div>
13822	        </div>
13823	      )}
13824	    </div>
13825	  );
13826	}
13827	
13828	// ============================================================================
13829	// Empty state for Report Viewers whose `assignedVessels` is empty.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-30 09:52:25Z)**_

Build green (3.32s, Vite — not Next.js, so the suggested skill doesn't apply here).

**Mobile-first fixes to Gov/Hospital Approvals (docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx ~L13577–13830):**

- **Header**: dropped pointless `flex justify-between` wrapper; title scales `text-xl md:text-2xl`, tighter `mb-4 md:mb-6`.
- **KPI tiles**: shortened `Pool utilization (upcoming)` → `Pool utilization · upcoming` (label/sub split), value scales `text-lg md:text-2xl` with `leading-tight break-words` so `0/15 seats` no longer overflows the 2-col tiles at 390px width.
- **Filters bar**: was a `flex flex-wrap` row of three narrow selects that crammed/wrapped on phone. Now `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3` → full-width stacked selects on mobile, inline row on ≥ sm. Selects bumped to `h-10` on mobile for tap comfort.
- **Card action buttons**: `h-10` → `h-11` (iOS 44pt tap target), icon size 14 → 16, added `active:scale-[0.98]` press feedback.
- **Reject modal**: converted to bottom sheet on mobile (`items-end sm:items-center`, `rounded-t-2xl sm:rounded-2xl`, `max-h-[90vh] overflow-y-auto`), preset reason chips bumped from `text-xs px-2 py-1` to `text-xs sm:text-sm px-3 py-2 rounded-lg`, textarea `text-xs h-20` → `text-sm h-24`, footer buttons now `grid grid-cols-2` (h-11) on mobile and inline `h-9` on desktop.

---

Desktop now shows cards in a 2-column grid instead of a cramped 10-column table — everything from the old table (Ref, Voyage, Passenger, Agency/Designation, ID, Reason, Officer, Submitted, Status, Action) is now in scannable cards. At ≥ xl (1280px) it'll go to 3 columns.

**Summary of mobile-first refactor (`docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`):**
- Removed the desktop-only `<table>` block — cards are now the single source of truth at every viewport
- Card container is now `grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-3 gap-3` — 1 col mobile/tablet, 2 col laptop, 3 col wide desktop
- Bundle dropped ~3KB minified from removing the redundant table renderer
- Build 2.76s, no errors

Combined with the earlier edits, the page is now genuinely mobile-first all the way up:
1. Compact title (subtitle hides on mobile)
2. Pill-tab status filter with live counts
3. Collapsible **Summary** + **Filters** disclosures (mobile only)
4. Responsive **card grid** for the list itself

1	---

9	
10	The deployed mockup at https://fandsbookingapp-mockup.vercel.app/ is built from `docs/vercel_mockup/` (Vite + React app, main file `src/MOCKUP.jsx`). The repo root has no `package.json` and no app code.
11	
12	The Vercel project `fandsbookingapp-mockup` (id `prj_0WF5MHE0LKQuTgW9WZL7TDr9OpEM`) now has:
13	- **Root Directory**: `docs/vercel_mockup`
14	- **Framework**: `vite`
15	- Linked to GitHub repo `bonitobonita24/FerryBook`, production branch `main`
16	
17	**Why:** Before this was set, every `git push` to main triggered a GitHub auto-build from the repo root, which had no app — it produced an empty/broken deployment that got auto-promoted to production and served HTTP 404 on the public alias. That caused the recurring "Vercel deployment is 404 again" incidents (see prior memory observations 4737, 5606, 5616, 5693 and the May 28 fix at 11pm GMT+8).
18	
19	**How to apply:**
20	- For CLI deploys with the Root Directory set, run `npx vercel --prod` from the **repo root** (with the project's `.vercel/` linked there), NOT from inside `docs/vercel_mockup/`. Running from inside the subdir stacks the paths and yields `docs/vercel_mockup/docs/vercel_mockup does not exist`.
21	- The `.vercel/project.json` lives at `docs/vercel_mockup/.vercel/project.json`. To deploy from repo root, temporarily copy `.vercel/` up, deploy, then remove it: `cp -r docs/vercel_mockup/.vercel .vercel && npx vercel --prod --yes && rm -rf .vercel`.
22	- If the production alias returns 404 again despite a "Ready" deployment, run a fresh `vercel --prod` — past incidents have shown stale/corrupt aliased deployments serving 404 even when marked Ready.
23	- Related: [[feedback-meeting-minutes-no-mobile-frame]] (other constraints on mockup screens).
24
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-30 11:23:05Z)**_

Deployed: status `READY` on the production alias. Hard-reload on your phone and the Agency / ID rows will be stacked vertically, no more side-by-side compression.

Recap of the fix (`docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`, mobile card meta block):
- `grid grid-cols-2` → `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2` so Agency and ID stack on phones and only go side-by-side at ≥ 640px.
- Added `break-words` on agency/designation/ID-type and `break-all` on the ID number so nothing overflows the card boundary at narrow widths.

Outer card list was already `grid-cols-1` at all mobile widths (multi-column only kicks in at `lg`/`xl` desktop), so cards themselves were never being packed horizontally on phone — the compression was happening inside each card.

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
---
name: feedback-mobile-first-strict
description: User defines "mobile-first" strictly — vertical stacking everywhere on phones, no inner side-by-side grids inside cards, no horizontal card grids on mobile, no desktop tables masquerading as a mobile layout
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

When the user asks for a mobile-first screen in the FerryBook mockup, this is what they expect — checked iteratively in 2026-05-30 GMT+8 session:

1. **Cards must stack vertically on mobile**, never side-by-side. Outer container should be `grid grid-cols-1` until at least `lg` (1024px) — only widen to 2/3 cols at `lg:`/`xl:`.
2. **No inner side-by-side grids inside a card on mobile.** Meta blocks like "Agency | ID" must be `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2`, not plain `grid-cols-2`. Long agency names like "DPWH - District Engineering Office" wrap to 3 lines and visibly overlap the neighbor column on narrow phones (~360px), which they explicitly call out as "fonts overlapping the boundaries".
3. **No `hidden md:block` desktop tables** parallel to the card view. Drop the table; let one responsive card layout handle every viewport.
4. **Replace dropdown filters with mobile-native controls** — pill-tab segmented control for primary status (with live counts), collapsible disclosures for secondary filters and KPI summary.
5. **44pt touch targets** (`h-11`) on primary actions, bottom-sheet modals on mobile (`items-end sm:items-center`, `rounded-t-2xl sm:rounded-2xl`).
6. Apply `break-words` / `break-all` on long content (agency names, ID numbers) so nothing escapes the card boundary.

**Why:** User tested on their actual Chrome on phone (not just the mockup's iPhone-frame preview) and rejected multiple iterations that *looked* mobile-friendly in dev devtools but compressed content on real narrow viewports. They are precise — "force to display in horizontal view because it will compress" means inner content too, not just outer columns.

**How to apply:** Any time you build or refactor a screen marked mobile-first in this repo, walk the DOM top-down and check every `grid-cols-N` / `flex` row at mobile width — including ones nested inside cards. Default everything to vertical stacking; only opt into horizontal layout with an explicit `sm:` / `md:` / `lg:` breakpoint. Verify on a 360px viewport, not just 390px iPhone preview.

Related: [[feedback-meeting-minutes-no-mobile-frame]] (avoid the iPhone-frame shell for natively responsive routes).

```
